---
title: "U.S. Labor Market Trends (2020–2023)"
author: "Shahlan Finchan + Group 3"
date: "2026-02-18"
output: html_document
runtime: shiny
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(shiny)

## Read Excel file for all of Table 9 from 2020 to 2023
t9_2020 <- read_excel(
  "2020 Table 9.xlsx", 
  col_types = c("text", "text", "text", "text", "text", "text")
)

t9_2021 <- read_excel(
  "2021_Table_9-2.xlsx",
  col_types = c("text","text","text","text","text","text")
)

t9_2022 <- read_excel(
  "2022_Table_9.xlsx",
  col_types = c("text","text","text","text","text","text")
)

t9_2023 <- read_excel(
  "2023_Table_9.xlsx",
  col_types = c("text","text","text","text","text","text")
)


# 2. Convert numeric columns from text to numbers for 2020 to 2023
t9_2020 <- t9_2020 %>%
  mutate(across(-Occupation, readr::parse_number))

t9_2021 <- t9_2021 %>%
  mutate(across(-Occupation, readr::parse_number))

t9_2022 <- t9_2022 %>%
  mutate(across(-Occupation, readr::parse_number))

t9_2023 <- t9_2023 %>%
  mutate(across(-Occupation, readr::parse_number))

# 3. Pivot longer to create tidy format
t9_2020_tidy <- t9_2020 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Occupation,
    names_to = c("sex","age"),
    names_sep = "_",
    values_to = "employment"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    age = as.numeric(age),
    year = 2020
  )   

t9_2021_tidy <- t9_2021 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Occupation,
    names_to = c("sex","age"),
    names_sep = "_",
    values_to = "employment"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    age = as.numeric(age),
    year = 2021
  )

t9_2022_tidy <- t9_2022 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Occupation,
    names_to = c("sex","age"),
    names_sep = "_",
    values_to = "employment"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    age = as.numeric(age),
    year = 2022
  )

t9_2023_tidy <- t9_2023 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Occupation,
    names_to = c("sex","age"),
    names_sep = "_",
    values_to = "employment"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    age = as.numeric(age),
    year = 2023
  )
# Combine all years
t9_tidy <- bind_rows(
  t9_2020_tidy,
  t9_2021_tidy,
  t9_2022_tidy,
  t9_2023_tidy
)

# 4. Inspect tidy data
glimpse(t9_tidy)


```

```{r} 
# Implementing Shiny with drop downs
selectInput("sex_input", "Select Sex:", choices = unique(t9_tidy$sex))
selectInput("age_input", "Select Age:", choices = unique(t9_tidy$age))
selectInput("year_input", "Select Year:", choices = unique(t9_tidy$year))

# Filtered table
renderTable({
  req(input$sex_input, input$age_input)
  t9_tidy %>%
    filter(sex == input$sex_input, age == input$age_input)
})
```

```{r} 
## Practice Histogram  
ggplot(t9_tidy %>% filter(sex != "Total"), 
       aes(x = employment, fill = sex)) +
  geom_histogram(position = "dodge", bins = 20, alpha = 0.8) +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Employment by Sex") +
  xlab("Employment Count") +
  ylab("Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()
```

The data is right-skewed, indicating that most individuals fall in the lower employment count brackets.
